title: GraphQL Resolver Database Connections
description: Establishes database connectivity for all 50+ GraphQL resolvers in the Apex platform using Neon PostgreSQL and Drizzle ORM. The resolvers are already implemented - this plan focuses on verification, error handling improvements, and comprehensive testing.

discovery_notes.database_connection: COMPLETE - Configured in src/lib/db/index.ts using Neon serverless + Drizzle with lazy initialization proxy pattern
discovery_notes.graphql_resolvers: COMPLETE - All 50+ resolvers in src/lib/graphql/schema.ts use Drizzle ORM with database queries
discovery_notes.drizzle_schemas: COMPLETE - 21 schema files in src/lib/db/schema/ covering: brands, brandMentions, audits, content, recommendations, organizations, users, apiIntegrations, geoScoreHistory, recommendationFeedback, and more
discovery_notes.error_handling: PARTIAL - Some resolvers have error handling, others need try-catch blocks added
discovery_notes.unit_tests: NOT STARTED - No GraphQL resolver tests exist yet
discovery_notes.integration_tests: NOT STARTED - No GraphQL integration tests exist yet
discovery_notes.existing_tests: Basic test files exist in tests/ (ai/, api/) - provides patterns to follow

current_state_analysis.context_pattern: GraphQL Yoga context provides auth via createContext()

phases.name: Discovery & Verification
phases.description: Verify existing database connections and resolver implementations work correctly

subtasks.title: Verify database connection initialization
subtasks.description: Confirm Neon client and Drizzle ORM initialize correctly with DATABASE_URL environment variable
acceptance_criteria: Database connection uses Neon serverless HTTP driver
Lazy initialization pattern prevents build-time errors
Schema is properly exported for use in resolvers
subtasks.notes: Verified: 1) Neon serverless HTTP driver used via @neondatabase/serverless + drizzle-orm/neon-http; 2) Lazy initialization via Proxy pattern prevents build-time errors; 3) Schema exports db, getDb(), and schema, imported correctly in GraphQL resolvers. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Audit existing resolver implementations
subtasks.description: Review all Query and Mutation resolvers in GraphQL schema to verify database connectivity
acceptance_criteria: All Query resolvers use db.select() pattern
All Mutation resolvers use db.insert/update/delete with .returning()
No static mock data arrays remain in resolvers
Field resolvers properly query related data
subtasks.notes: Comprehensive audit of all 54 resolvers verified: 1) 20/22 Query resolvers use db.select() pattern (2 intentional static: subscriptionPlans, health); 2) 18/23 Mutation resolvers use db.insert/update/delete with .returning() (5 intentional stubs for external services); 3) No unintentional static mock data arrays remain; 4) All 11 field resolvers properly query related data from database. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Verify Drizzle schema completeness
subtasks.description: Ensure all GraphQL types have corresponding Drizzle schema definitions
acceptance_criteria: Schema tables exist for: brands, brandMentions, audits, content, recommendations, organizations, apiIntegrations, geoScoreHistory, recommendationFeedback
All schema types are exported from index.ts
subtasks.notes:
Comprehensive verification completed. All GraphQL types have corresponding Drizzle schema definitions:

**Schema Mapping Verified:**

2. Mention → schema.brandMentions (mentions.ts) ✓
3. GEOScore → Computed on-the-fly from brandMentions + recommendations ✓

11. User → schema.users (users.ts) ✓

**Relations Verified:**

- recommendationsRelations → brand, audit, assignedTo
- recommendationFeedbackRelations → recommendation

- auditsRelations → brand, triggeredBy

**Exports Verified:**
All tables, relations, types, and enums properly exported from src/lib/db/schema/index.ts

All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.description: Create mapping documentation showing which resolvers use which schema tables

All Mutation resolvers documented with DB status

Comprehensive resolver-to-schema mapping documentation created in build-progress.txt. Includes:
- 22 Query resolvers mapped to schema tables with DB operations, auth requirements, and line numbers
- 23 Mutation resolvers mapped to schema tables with DB operations

- Total: 56 resolvers (49 database-connected, 7 static/external)
All acceptance criteria met.

phases.name: Error Handling Enhancement
phases.description: Add robust try-catch error handling to all database operations in resolvers

subtasks.title: Add try-catch to Query resolvers
subtasks.description: Wrap all Query resolver database calls in try-catch with user-friendly error messages
acceptance_criteria: All Query resolvers have try-catch blocks
Database errors are logged to console
Not-found cases return null or throw appropriate errors
subtasks.notes:
Successfully wrapped all 20 database-calling Query resolvers in try-catch blocks with user-friendly error messages. Each resolver now:
1. Wraps database operations in try-catch
2. Logs errors with console.error for debugging (with descriptive context)
3. Throws user-friendly GraphQL errors for clients
4. Handles not-found cases by returning null

Resolvers updated:
- Brand resolvers: brand, brands, brandByDomain
- Mention resolvers: mention, mentions  
- GEO Score resolvers: geoScore, geoScoreHistory
- Recommendation resolvers: recommendation, recommendations
- Audit resolvers: audit, audits
- Content resolvers: content, contents
- Subscription resolver: subscription
- Integration resolvers: integrations, integration
- Analytics resolvers: analytics, comparePlatforms, competitorAnalysis
- User resolver: me

Note: subscriptionPlans and health resolvers excluded as they are static with no database calls.

Commit: 087afb51 - Verified TypeScript compilation passes for schema.ts.

subtasks.title: Add try-catch to Mutation resolvers
subtasks.description: Wrap all Mutation resolver database calls in try-catch with user-friendly error messages
acceptance_criteria: All Mutation resolvers have try-catch blocks
Foreign key violations provide helpful messages
subtasks.notes: All Mutation resolver database calls are now wrapped in try-catch blocks with user-friendly error messages. Implemented error handling for 17 mutations with DB calls: markMentionReviewed, calculateGeoScore, updateRecommendationStatus, assignRecommendation, submitRecommendationFeedback, startAudit, cancelAudit, createContent, updateContent, deleteContent, optimizeContent, publishContent, changePlan, cancelSubscription, connectIntegration, disconnectIntegration, syncIntegration. Excluded stubs without DB calls (refreshMentions, generateRecommendations, createCheckoutSession). Pattern: console.error for logging, re-throw user-friendly Error messages, preserve specific errors like "not found". Verified with 41 try blocks and 40 catch blocks across all resolvers.

subtasks.title: Add try-catch to field resolvers
subtasks.description: Wrap Brand, Mention, Recommendation, Audit, Content field resolvers in try-catch
acceptance_criteria: Brand.mentions, Brand.recommendations, Brand.audits have error handling
Mention.brand, Recommendation.brand, Audit.brand, Content.brand have error handling
subtasks.notes:
All 11 field resolvers are now wrapped in try-catch blocks with error handling:

**Brand field resolvers:**
- Brand.mentions: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand mentions" error
- Brand.recommendations: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand recommendations" error
- Brand.audits: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand audits" error
- Brand.geoScore: try-catch with "Failed to calculate brand GEO score" error
- Brand.stats: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand stats" error

**Entity field resolvers:**
- Mention.brand: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand for mention" error
- Recommendation.brand: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand for recommendation" error
- Recommendation.feedback: try-catch with "Failed to fetch recommendation feedback" error
- Audit.brand: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand for audit" error
- Content.brand: try-catch with "Failed to fetch brand for content" error
- GEOScore.history: try-catch with "Failed to fetch GEO score history" error

Pattern: console.error for debugging + throw user-friendly Error messages.
Total try-catch blocks in schema.ts: 51 (covering all resolvers with DB operations).
All acceptance criteria met. Commit: 6ee34bef

subtasks.title: Create database error utility
subtasks.description: Create a utility function for consistent database error handling and logging
subtasks.notes:
Created src/lib/graphql/db-error-handler.ts with comprehensive error handling utilities:

**Functions Created:**
1. `handleDatabaseError(error, config)` - Main error handler with:
   - Error classification (unique_constraint, foreign_key, not_null, check_constraint, connection, timeout)
   - Automatic console.error logging with operation context
   - User-friendly error messages based on error type

2. `handleNotFound(result, entityType, options)` - Not-found handler with:
   - Optional throwIfNotFound behavior (default: true)
   - Returns null or throws based on configuration
   - Includes entityId in error messages

3. `handleConstraintViolation(error, config)` - Constraint handler with:
   - Custom messages for uniqueFields, foreignKeyMessage, checkConstraintMessage
   - Returns false if not a constraint violation (allows fallthrough)
   - Throws user-friendly messages for constraint violations

4. `isKnownError(error)` - Identifies known errors that should be re-thrown

5. `classifyDatabaseError(error)` - Classifies database errors into DatabaseErrorType

6. `withErrorHandling(resolverFn, config)` - Higher-order function for resolver wrapping

7. `logDatabaseOperation(operation, details)` - Debug logging utility

**Schema Updates:**
Updated key resolvers to use utility functions:
- Query: brand, brands, mention (demonstrating handleNotFound, handleDatabaseError, isKnownError)
- Mutation: createBrand, updateBrand, deleteBrand (demonstrating handleConstraintViolation)

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
- ✅ handleDatabaseError function created
- ✅ handleNotFound function created  
- ✅ handleConstraintViolation function created
- ✅ Functions used consistently across key resolvers

Commit: 46efdb33 - TypeScript/ESLint verification passed

phases.name: Unit Tests
phases.description: Create unit tests for resolver logic with mocked database

subtasks.title: Create test setup and mocks
subtasks.description: Set up test infrastructure with Vitest mocks for Drizzle database client
acceptance_criteria: Mock factory functions for test data
subtasks.notes:
Test infrastructure for GraphQL resolver testing successfully set up with Vitest mocks:

**Files Created:**
1. `tests/graphql/mocks/db.ts` - Drizzle database mock with chainable query builder (select/insert/update/delete), call tracking, and assertion utilities
2. `tests/graphql/factories/index.ts` - Factory functions for all entity types (brands, mentions, recommendations, audits, content, organizations, users, integrations, GEO score history, GraphQL context)
3. `tests/graphql/setup.ts` - Main test setup with setupGraphQLTest(), createClerkMock(), DatabaseErrors helpers, and testResolver/testFieldResolver utilities
4. `tests/graphql/setup.test.ts` - 36 verification tests (all passing)

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
- ✅ Drizzle db mock created (createDbMock)
- ✅ Schema tables mocked (createSchemaMock with all tables)
- ✅ Test setup file configured (setupGraphQLTest)
- ✅ Mock factory functions for test data (all entity factories)

Commit: 14133a9a

subtasks.title: Create Brand resolver tests
subtasks.description: Test brand Query and Mutation resolvers including brand, brands, brandByDomain, createBrand, updateBrand, deleteBrand
acceptance_criteria: brands query handles pagination and search
subtasks.notes:
Brand resolver unit tests implemented and verified (46 tests passing):

**Query Tests (13 tests):**
- `brand`: fetch by ID, null for non-existent, organization access verification, data transformation
- `brands`: paginated list, default/custom limit, search filter, empty results, pagination info, cursor format
- `brandByDomain`: domain lookup, null when not found, organization filtering

**Mutation Tests (17 tests):**
- `createBrand`: insert with .returning(), organizationId from context, competitors transformation, platforms mapping, isActive default, optional null fields, unique constraint handling
- `updateBrand`: ownership verification, .returning() usage, partial updates, unauthorized access, not found error, updatedAt timestamp, isActive updates
- `deleteBrand`: ownership verification, successful delete, not found returns false, unauthorized access, foreign key constraints

**Additional Tests (16 tests):**
- Error handling: connection errors, timeouts, not-null constraints, error logging
- Authentication: requireAuth(), userId fallback
- Field transformations: monitoringPlatforms → platforms, competitor name extraction, null handling

Commit: 4587c23c - Tests follow existing patterns from tests/graphql/setup.test.ts

subtasks.title: Create Mention resolver tests
subtasks.description: Test mention Query resolvers and related mutations
acceptance_criteria: mention query returns correct data with computed fields
mentions query handles filters (brandId, platform, sentiment)
subtasks.notes:
Mention resolver unit tests implemented and verified (46 tests passing):

**Query Tests (20 tests):**
- `mention`: fetch by ID, null for non-existent, compute sentimentScore (0.8/0.2/0.5 for positive/negative/neutral), compute isRecommendation from promptCategory, compute competitorMentioned from competitors array, required fields verification
- `mentions`: fetch all, filter by brandId/platform/sentiment, default limit of 20, custom limits, empty results, orderBy timestamp desc, pagination info, cursor format

**Mutation Tests (7 tests):**
- `markMentionReviewed`: fetch with computed fields, error when not found, re-throw known errors, compute competitorMentioned
- `refreshMentions`: stub structure validation, brandId and optional platforms parameters

**Field Resolver Tests (4 tests):**
- `Mention.brand`: fetch brand for mention, null when not found, transform with platforms array, extract competitor names

**Edge Cases and Utilities (15 tests):**
- Computed fields edge cases: mixed/undefined sentiment, null metadata/url/position
- Platform filtering: all valid platform values
- Sentiment filtering: all valid sentiment values  
- Pagination: cursor encoding/decoding, hasNextPage, startCursor/endCursor, null cursors for empty
- Error handling: connection errors, timeouts, error logging, user-friendly messages

Commit: 32096f68 - Tests follow existing patterns from brands.test.ts

subtasks.title: Create GEO Score resolver tests
subtasks.description: Test geoScore and geoScoreHistory queries and calculateGeoScore mutation
subtasks.notes:
GEO Score resolver unit tests implemented and verified (56 tests passing):

**Query Tests (17 tests):**
- `geoScore`: Calculate score from mention/recommendation counts, visibility/sentiment/recommendation score calculations, weighted formula, authentication, edge cases (zero mentions, high mentions)
- `geoScoreHistory`: Fetch history by brandId, default 30-day limit, custom days parameter, date filtering, ordering by calculatedAt desc, data transformation

**Mutation Tests (14 tests):**
- `calculateGeoScore`: Visibility from mention count, sentiment from positive/negative ratio, recommendation from completed/total ratio, previous score lookup, trend calculation (up/down/stable), insert into geoScoreHistory, return structure, authentication

**Field Resolver Tests (5 tests):**
- `GEOScore.history`: Fetch history for parent.brandId, default/custom days, data transformation with platformScores

**Additional Tests (20 tests):**
- Score Calculation Formula: Weighted formula verification, rounding, edge cases
- Error Handling: Connection errors, timeouts, error logging, user-friendly messages, foreign key violations
- Date Range Filtering: 7/30/90 day ranges
- Trend Analysis: Up/down/stable identification
- Component Score Calculations: Visibility capping, sentiment percentage, recommendation completion

Commit: 5c66cecf - Tests follow existing patterns from brands.test.ts and mentions.test.ts
